title: Reproduce the human mitochondrial AT skew diagram in the web app
summary: Build a single-record circular mitochondrial genome diagram with custom circular track slots for GC content, GC skew, AT skew, ticks, labels, and a left legend.
estimatedTime: 5-10 min after the input is available
primaryWorkflow: Circular custom track slots with AT skew
requirements: The human mitochondrial GenBank flat file listed below.
Use a local copy of gbdraw.app or the hosted web app; genome processing runs in the browser.

downloads.label: NC_012920
downloads.note: Download the complete human mitochondrial genome as a GenBank flat file and save it with this filename if you want to match the Gallery session.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Circular mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Circular mode before adding the mitochondrial GenBank input.

operations.title: Mode segmented control
operations.body: Select Circular in the mode segmented control.

media.alt: Mode segmented control with Circular selected.
media.caption: Select Circular in the mode segmented control.

manualSteps.title: Upload the GenBank file
manualSteps.body: Upload HmmtDNA.gbk. The Gallery session uses the human mitochondrial reference record NC_012920.1.

operations.title: Circular GenBank uploader
operations.body: Upload HmmtDNA.gbk so the file chip is visible.

media.alt: Circular GenBank uploader with the HmmtDNA.gbk file chip.
media.caption: Upload HmmtDNA.gbk in the Circular GenBank uploader.

manualSteps.title: Set the basic circular layout
manualSteps.body: Use the same compact circular defaults as the Gallery figure.
items: Features: CDS, rRNA, tRNA, tmRNA, ncRNA, repeat_region

operations.title: Track preset selector
operations.body: Open Track Preset and choose Middle.

media.alt: Layout card with the Track Preset menu open and the Middle option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Track Preset in the Layout card and choose the highlighted Middle option.

actions.script: const app = window.__GBDRAW_APP__; if (app?.adv) app.adv.circular_track_slots_enabled = false;

operations.title: Label mode selector
operations.body: Open Label Mode and choose Out.

media.alt: Labels card with the Label Mode menu open and the Out option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Label Mode in the Labels card and choose the highlighted Out option.

operations.title: Legend position selector
operations.body: Open Legend Position and choose Left.

media.alt: Title and Legend card with the Legend Position menu open and the Left option highlighted.
media.caption: Open Legend Position in the Title & Legend card and choose the highlighted Left option.

manualSteps.title: Set the dinucleotide calculation window
manualSteps.body: Use a short mitochondrial window so GC content, GC skew, and AT skew all show local structure.

operations.title: Dinucleotide window controls
operations.body: Set Dinucleotide to GC, Window to 500, and Step to 50.

media.alt: Dinucleotide content/skew controls set to Window 500, Step 50, and Dinucleotide GC.
media.caption: Set Dinucleotide to GC, Window to 500, and Step to 50.

manualSteps.title: Open Custom Track Slots
manualSteps.body: In the circular layout controls, enable Custom Track Slots and reset to the Middle template if you need a clean starting stack.
manualSteps.note: Axis index 0 keeps all listed slots on the single circular axis.

operations.title: Custom Track Slots panel
operations.body: Open Custom Track Slots so the Middle stack controls are visible.

media.alt: Custom Track Slots panel open in the circular Layout card.
media.caption: Open Custom Track Slots in the Layout card.

operations.title: Middle template slot stack
operations.body: Use the Middle template stack with the feature, GC content, and GC skew slots visible.

media.alt: Radial track stack showing feature, GC content, and GC skew slots from the Middle template.
media.caption: Use the Middle template radial track stack with feature, GC content, and GC skew slots visible.

manualSteps.title: Add the AT skew track
manualSteps.body: Add another Dinucleotide skew row inside the axis and use it for AT skew rather than the default GC skew.

operations.title: Add AT skew slot
operations.body: Set the added dinucleotide skew slot to AT, label it AT skew, and keep the two custom colors.

media.alt: Custom circular track slot a_skew_2 set to dinucleotide AT with AT skew legend colors.
media.caption: Set the added dinucleotide skew slot to AT and label it AT skew.

manualSteps.title: Add the tick track
manualSteps.body: Keep a Ticks row inside the axis so position labels sit inside the tick marks.

operations.title: Tick track slot
operations.body: Keep the tick slot in the custom stack and set the tick layout to Labels inside and ticks outside.

media.alt: Custom circular tick slot set to Labels inside and ticks outside.
media.caption: Keep the tick slot in the custom circular track stack.

manualSteps.title: Prioritize gene labels for CDS features
manualSteps.body: Set qualifier priority for CDS labels to gene so compact labels such as ND1, COX1, ATP6, and CYTB are used instead of long product names.

operations.title: CDS label priority controls
operations.body: Set the CDS qualifier priority rule to gene in the Labels card.

media.alt: Qualifier Priority controls with a CDS rule prioritizing gene.
media.caption: Set CDS label priority to gene in the Labels card.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The preview should show the labeled mitochondrial feature ring, GC content, GC skew, AT skew, and a left legend that includes AT skew (+) and AT skew (-).

operations.title: Generated AT skew preview
operations.body: Inspect the generated preview with the custom track stack and left legend.

media.alt: Actual human mitochondrial AT skew tutorial UI showing the generated AT skew preview with the custom track stack and left legend.
media.caption: The generated preview includes the custom track stack and left legend.

manualSteps.title: Inspect a feature
manualSteps.body: Click a CDS or RNA feature in the interactive SVG to inspect its gene, product, location, and sequence metadata.

operations.title: Feature popup
operations.body: Click the rendered feature or match so the Feature popup metadata is visible.

media.alt: Zoomed human mitochondrial AT skew crop with the clicked RNR2 rRNA feature and feature popup metadata.
media.caption: The zoomed crop keeps the clicked RNR2 feature and its gene, product, and db_xref metadata readable.

colorRules.title: This example does not need feature color rules
colorRules.body: The main tutorial point is the custom numeric track stack. Feature colors use the ajisai palette defaults.

colorRules.title: Use gene for compact labels
colorRules.body: For mitochondrial CDS labels, gene is usually more readable than product because product names are long and repetitive.

colorRules.title: Separate feature colors from skew colors
colorRules.body: Feature color rules affect CDS, rRNA, tRNA, and other genome features. GC skew and AT skew colors are set on the Dinucleotide skew slot rows.

colorRules.title: Name custom numeric tracks through legend labels
colorRules.body: Use the slot legend label AT skew so the legend shows AT skew (+) and AT skew (-) instead of another generic skew entry.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click features before changing labels
postGenerationEdits.body: Use the feature popup to inspect gene, product, note, and db_xref values before changing qualifier priority or label overrides.

operations.title: Feature popup
operations.body: Click the rendered feature or match so the Feature popup metadata is visible.

media.alt: Zoomed human mitochondrial AT skew crop with the clicked RNR2 rRNA feature and feature popup metadata.
media.caption: The clicked RNR2 feature and popup metadata support label and qualifier-priority checks.

postGenerationEdits.title: Review the generated legend
postGenerationEdits.body: Generate Diagram already adds AT skew (+) and AT skew (-) to the legend. Use the legend editor only for optional caption/order tweaks; the two entries represent positive and negative skew.

operations.title: Generated legend entries
operations.body: Open the Legend drawer to review the generated AT skew entries.

media.alt: Legend editor drawer with AT skew legend entries visible.
media.caption: The Legend drawer shows the AT skew entries generated with the diagram.

postGenerationEdits.title: Export the figure
postGenerationEdits.body: Download SVG for editable vector output. Use PNG or PDF for quick sharing or draft figures.

operations.title: Export controls
operations.body: Use the Preview export toolbar to choose SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, or PDF.

media.alt: Actual Circular Preview export toolbar with the DPI selector and SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export buttons visible.
media.caption: The Preview export toolbar shows DPI plus SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Save a session
postGenerationEdits.body: Save a .gbdraw-session.json after edits so the web app can restore the input, custom track slots, generated output, and SVG adjustments.

operations.title: Session save controls
operations.body: Click Save Session in the web app header toolbar.

media.alt: Actual Circular web app header toolbar with Save Session, Load Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The app header toolbar shows Save Session in the same toolbar used to load sessions.

troubleshooting.title: AT skew is missing from the legend
troubleshooting.body: Check that the added slot uses renderer Dinucleotide skew, dinucleotide AT, and legend label AT skew.

troubleshooting.title: The skew rings overlap labels or ticks
troubleshooting.body: Check that Custom Track Slots are enabled, reset to Middle if needed, and keep GC content, GC skew, AT skew, and ticks inside the axis.

troubleshooting.title: Labels show long product names
troubleshooting.body: Check that the CDS qualifier priority rule uses gene before regenerating the diagram.

troubleshooting.title: The generated diagram differs from the Gallery image
troubleshooting.body: Check input record NC_012920.1, track type middle, labels out, legend left, window 500, step 50, AT skew colors, and the tick label layout.
